Title: Sleep Deprivation and Reaction Time by Baseline Speed — Belenky et al. 2003
Source: Belenky, G. et al. (2003). Patterns of performance degradation and restoration during sleep restriction. Journal of Sleep Research, 12(1), 1–12. R lme4::sleepstudy.
note: Late-period observations (days 6–9, high deprivation) produce more slow responses (>350ms) than early-period observations (days 0–3) in both fast-baseline and slow-baseline subject groups. Baseline speed confounds the grouping but does not reverse the deprivation effect. Slow-baseline subjects show higher impairment rates (46.9%) than fast-baseline subjects (32.5%) during late days, confirming rather than reversing the pooled result.

treatments.Description: Sleep deprivation days 0–3 (baseline to mild deprivation)
Sleep deprivation days 6–9 (moderate to severe deprivation)

strata.Description: Subjects with mean baseline reaction time < 270ms
Subjects with mean baseline reaction time >= 270ms
